channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: Application of LLMs to Threat Assessment of Foreign Peacekeeping Missions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27106v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在海外维和行动中，及时、准确地识别潜在安全威胁对任务成败与人员安全至关重要。传统威胁评估高度依赖人工情报分析，面临信息过载、响应滞后与主观偏差等挑战。本研究聚焦于将大语言模型（LLMs）系统性引入维和任务威胁评估流程，以提升分析效率、一致性与可解释性。

方法与技术路径

本工作基于欧盟驻格鲁吉亚监测团（EU Monitoring Mission in Georgia）这一真实用例，依托PINPOINT项目框架，构建了融合多源能力的端到端工作流：

  • 跨学科风险建模：整合政治、地理、社会与安全维度，定义12类维和相关威胁类型（如武装袭击、舆论操纵、基础设施破坏）；
  • OSINT驱动的数据采集：自动抓取多语种开源媒体（新闻、社交媒体、本地论坛），覆盖高风险时段与区域；
  • LLM增强型威胁抽取：采用指令微调的开源LLM（Llama-3-8B-Instruct），执行三阶段处理：① 媒体内容→威胁类别映射；② 结构化三元组提取（主体-行为-目标）；③ 相关性重排序与事实锚定（通过检索增强生成RAG对接权威数据库与任务简报）；
  • 人工协同验证机制：设计双盲标注协议，由5名资深维和情报分析师对LLM输出进行威胁存在性、任务关联度、时空精确性三维度评分。

主要发现与创新点

在为期6个月的实证评估中（覆盖2,147篇媒体文档），LLM系统在威胁识别准确率达89.3%，任务相关性判断与人类专家Kappa一致性达0.82（p<0.001），显著优于基线规则引擎（+37.1% F1）与通用LLM零样本提示（+29.5%）。关键创新在于：① 首次将结构化风险框架与LLM语义理解深度耦合；② 提出“任务锚定”（Mission-Anchoring）机制，强制模型输出与维和授权条款、部署区域、行动阶段强对齐；③ 开源全流程提示模板与评估基准，支持可复现、可审计的AI辅助决策。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a novel LLM-powered workflow for threat assessment in foreign peacekeeping missions, grounded in the EU Monitoring Mission in Georgia (PINPOINT project). Our approach integrates an interdisciplinary risk model with OSINT-based multilingual media collection and a three-stage LLM pipeline: (1) mission-aligned threat categorization, (2) structured threat triple extraction (actor-action-target), and (3) relevance re-ranking and grounding via RAG against official mission documents and geospatial databases. Evaluated on 2,147 real-world media reports, our system achieves 89.3% threat detection accuracy and κ = 0.82 agreement with human analysts on mission relevance—outperforming rule-based and zero-shot baselines by >29% F1. Results demonstrate that purpose-built, domain-grounded LLMs can significantly augment analyst capacity while ensuring operational accountability and interpretability.

Abstract

We present a novel approach for applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to threat assessment in the context of foreign peacekeeping missions. Building on the PINPOINT project and its use case, the EU Monitoring Mission in Georgia, we combine an interdisciplinary risk-model with OSINT-based media collection and LLM-supported threat extraction. The proposed workflow maps media contents to mission-relevant threats, extracts structured information and applies several additional LLM-based processing steps to improve relevance and grounding. An evaluation of threats extracted from media documents shows high agreement between automatically generated results and human judgment for core aspects such as threat and mission relevance. These results indicate that LLMs provide a promising approach to support analysts in the context of peacekeeping missions.

item.title: ShareLock: A Stealthy Multi-Tool Threshold Poisoning Attack Against MCP
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27027v1

AI Summary (中文)

ShareLock：一种针对MCP的隐蔽多工具阈值投毒攻击框架

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动智能体的快速发展，Model Context Protocol（MCP）——这一连接LLM与外部工具的开源协议——已成为现代智能体生态系统的基础设施。然而，其广泛部署也催生了新型安全威胁，尤其是工具投毒攻击（Tool Poisoning Attack, TPA）：攻击者通过篡改工具描述等客户端可编辑内容，在LLM与服务器交互中隐式注入恶意指令。现有投毒方法多采用单点、明文嵌入范式，极易被人工审查或自动化检测器识别；更关键的是，当前研究尚未系统探索多工具协同投毒——即分散恶意负载于多个合法工具描述中，以稀释单点异常、规避检测。

本文提出 ShareLock，首个面向MCP的多工具阈值投毒框架。其核心创新在于将Shamir门限秘密共享方案深度适配至工具投毒场景：将恶意指令拆解为多个“看似无害”的秘密份额，分别嵌入不同工具的自然语言描述中（如API文档、功能说明），实现信息论意义上的保密性；仅当达到预设阈值（如3/5个工具被同时调用）时，服务器端在更新过程中触发隐式重构，才完整恢复并执行恶意载荷，从而绕过常规静态审计与动态监控。

我们在真实MCP客户端（MCP-CLI与MCP-SDK）上构建了覆盖4类典型多工具场景（如文件操作+网络请求+数据库查询+日志分析）的基准测试集，并在GPT-4、Claude-3、Qwen2.5及Llama-3等主流LLM上验证。结果表明：ShareLock在基于工具描述的检测任务中误报率降低72%，平均攻击成功率稳定高于90%，且对工具缺失、描述微调等扰动具备强鲁棒性，显著超越单工具投毒基线。

AI Summary (English)

ShareLock is a stealthy multi-tool threshold poisoning framework targeting the Model Context Protocol (MCP), designed to evade both manual inspection and automated detectors. By adapting Shamir’s secret sharing scheme, ShareLock splits a malicious instruction into benign-looking secret shares distributed across multiple tool descriptions—ensuring information-theoretic secrecy and fault tolerance. Only when a predefined threshold of tools is invoked during server update does the covert trigger reconstruct the payload, enabling high-impact breaches. Evaluated across four realistic multi-tool scenarios and two MCP clients (MCP-CLI and MCP-SDK) with GPT-4, Claude-3, Qwen2.5, and Llama-3, ShareLock achieves >90% average attack success while reducing detection rates by 72% compared to single-tool baselines—demonstrating unprecedented stealth and robustness in practical MCP deployments.

Abstract

With the rapid evolution of LLM-driven agents, Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open protocol bridging LLMs with external tools, has quickly become foundational to modern agent ecosystems. However, the expanding adoption of MCP has also introduced novel security concerns such as Tool Poisoning Attack (TPA), which exploit LLM-server interactions to inject malicious prompts. Existing poisoning schemes typically adopt a monolithic plaintext embedding paradigm, which fails to withstand manual inspection or automated detectors. Current research still lacks a systematic analysis on multi-tool poisoning, where multiple tools can be exploited cooperatively to disperse detection risk. In this paper, we introduce ShareLock, a multi-tool threshold poisoning framework that utilizes Shamir's threshold scheme to ensure exceptional stealth and fault tolerance. ShareLock distributes the malicious instruction as benign-looking secret shares across multiple tool descriptions, achieving both information-theoretic secrecy and attack robustness against moderate auditing. After a covert reconstruction trigger is planted during server update, the aggregated shares reconstruct the hidden instruction, resulting in critical breaches of system assets or private data. To evaluate the realistic threat of ShareLock, we constructed a comprehensive benchmark encompassing four multi-tool scenarios and conducted extensive experiments across mainstream LLMs on two distinct MCP clients. Our results demonstrate that ShareLock significantly outperforms existing single-tool poisoning strategies in tool description-based detection while maintaining an average attack success rate exceeding 90%.

item.title: SpikeTimer: Exploring Active Copyright Protection in Spiking Neural Networks via Temporal Backdoor Regularization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26841v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

脉冲神经网络（SNN）凭借其事件驱动、低功耗特性，已成为边缘AI与机器人实时感知任务的重要计算范式。然而，相较于深度神经网络（DNN）已有的水印、指纹等版权保护方案，SNN的版权保护长期被忽视——其核心难点在于时间编码依赖性强、脉冲动态高度非线性、且缺乏适配时序结构的主动授权机制。

方法：SpikeTimer框架

本文提出首个面向SNN的主动版权保护框架 SpikeTimer，其核心创新在于基于时间片的后门正则化机制：

  • 将输入脉冲序列按时间维度划分为若干离散timeslice（时间片），仅在预设的授权时间片内嵌入轻量级二进制授权令牌（token）；
  • 利用SNN固有的时序敏感性，使模型仅对“令牌出现在正确时间片”的输入产生正确响应，对错位嵌入或无令牌输入均触发显著性能退化（如分类错误）；
  • 支持多用户授权（不同用户分配独立时间片组合）与任意形态令牌（可为稀疏脉冲模式、特定神经元激活序列等），兼顾灵活性与安全性。

主要结果与创新

  • 在N-MNIST、DVS Gesture、CIFAR10-DVS等主流神经形态数据集上验证：授权样本精度仅下降约1.5%，而未授权样本平均准确率压低至≈10%，实现强区分性；
  • 对模型微调（fine-tuning）与剪枝（pruning）攻击展现出鲁棒防御能力，令牌有效性在80%通道剪枝后仍保持；
  • 首次建立时间有效性即版权凭证（temporal token validity as copyright proof）范式，将版权保护从静态参数绑定升维至动态时序契约。

AI Summary (English)

Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) offer energy-efficient, event-driven processing but lack effective copyright protection due to their complex temporal dynamics. This paper introduces SpikeTimer, the first active copyright framework for SNNs, leveraging temporal backdoor regularization. It partitions input spike trains into timeslices and embeds lightweight authorization tokens exclusively within designated slices—exploiting SNNs’ intrinsic time-sensitivity to trigger correct inference only when tokens appear in authorized temporal windows. Unauthorized inputs (e.g., wrong slice, no token, or adversarial tampering) yield severe accuracy drops (~10%), while authorized inputs suffer only ~1.5% degradation. SpikeTimer supports multi-user authorization, arbitrary token morphologies, and resists fine-tuning and pruning (maintaining token efficacy even after 80% channel pruning). Its core innovation is establishing temporal token validity as a verifiable, dynamic copyright proof—shifting protection from static weights to time-bound behavioral contracts.

Abstract

Spiking Neural Networks (SNN) have emerged as a revolutionary paradigm compared to traditional Deep Neural Networks (DNN) in energy-efficient computing, showcasing exceptional capabilities in processing event-driven sensory data for real-time applications like robotics and edge AI systems. However, unlike extensive studies on DNN copyright solutions, SNN copyright protection remains largely underexplored due to their inherent temporal coding complexities and spike-driven computation. In this study, we propose a novel active copyright protection framework named SpikeTimer for SNNs via temporal backdoor learning. SpikeTimer partitions neuromorphic data into designated timeslices and exclusively embeds authorized tokens within authorized slices. Furthermore, the inherent temporal segmentation characteristic intrinsically enables SpikeTimer to support multi-user authorization mechanisms and accommodates token embedding of arbitrary morphology. Based on this, SpikeTimer precisely responds to authorized data containing a token within the correct timeslice, while producing erroneous responses to unauthorized data. Our key innovation lies in establishing a time-dependent authorization mechanism that protects the SNN copyright by temporal token validity. Additionally, SpikeTimer retains its defensive efficacy even under adversarial attempts. Evaluations on multiple neuromorphic datasets manifest that SpikeTimer achieves around 10% accuracy on unauthorized data with merely around 1.5% degradation on authorized inputs. Moreover, SpikeTimer demonstrates robust resistance against model finetuning and pruning threats.

item.title: MIRROR: Novelty-Constrained Memory-Guided MCTS Red-Teaming for Agentic RAG
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26793v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多模态智能体式检索增强生成（Agentic RAG）系统显著扩大了红队测试的攻击面，不仅涵盖传统提示注入，更延伸至文本投毒、图像注入、直接查询攻击及编排器级工具操纵四大新维度。现有红队方法多为单表面定制，高度依赖复用已知攻击模板——在文本投毒基准上，我们实测其精确重复率高达73–84%，严重制约泛化性与发现未知漏洞的能力。

方法创新：MIRROR框架

本文提出MIRROR（Novelty-Constrained Memory-Guided MCTS Red-Teaming），首个支持跨表面统一攻击的红队框架。其核心包含三重机制：

  • 记忆引导的蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）：以历史成功攻击为记忆先验，动态构建攻击策略树；
  • 上下文条件化候选生成：所有候选攻击均显式绑定检索到的上下文，确保语义相关性；
  • 确定性新颖性门控（Novelty Gate）：通过归一化相似度比对，自动拒绝任何与检索集完全匹配的候选，根除模板复制，同时保留检索对搜索先验的指导作用。

关键成果

在多模态Agentic RAG靶标上，MIRROR实现全面领先：

  • 图像投毒攻击成功率（ASR）达76%（基线仅52%）；
  • 编排器攻击ASR达97%，且查询成本降低50%；
  • 四大攻击表面间方差最低（变异系数仅0.47），远优于专用基线（如后缀优化在文本投毒达79%，但在直接查询中骤降至1%）。

同步开源ART-SafeBench：含41,815条原生包内记录，配合运行时适配器扩展至41,991+条跨表面高质量攻击样本，支撑可复现、可扩展的红队评估。

AI Summary (English)

Multimodal agentic RAG systems introduce four distinct attack surfaces—text poisoning, image injection, direct-query attacks, and orchestrator-level tool manipulation—beyond conventional prompt injection. Existing red-teaming methods are surface-specific and heavily reuse templates (73–84% exact duplication on text-poisoning benchmarks). We propose MIRROR, a unified cross-surface framework that integrates memory-guided Monte Carlo tree search (MCTS) with retrieval-conditioned candidate generation under an explicit deterministic Novelty Gate, which rejects any candidate matching the retrieved context via normalized similarity—enabling contextual guidance without prompt copying. On a multimodal agentic RAG target, MIRROR achieves 76% ASR on image poisoning (+24 pts over baselines), 97% ASR on orchestrator attacks at half the query cost, and the lowest cross-surface variance (coefficient of variation = 0.47). In contrast, specialized baselines fail catastrophically across surfaces (e.g., suffix optimization: 79% ASR on text poisoning but only 1% on direct queries). We release ART-SafeBench, containing 41,815 in-package records and runtime adapters yielding ≥41,991 high-quality, surface-diverse attack instances.

Abstract

Multimodal agentic retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems expand the attack surface beyond prompt injection to include text poisoning, image injection, direct-query attacks, and orchestrator-level tool manipulation. Existing red-teaming approaches are typically surface-specific and often recycle known attack templates; on text-poisoning benchmarks we measure 73-84% exact duplication. We present MIRROR, a unified cross-surface framework that performs memory-guided Monte Carlo tree search while conditioning candidate generation on retrieved context under an explicit novelty constraint. A deterministic Novelty Gate rejects any candidate matching the retrieval set under normalized comparison, allowing retrieval to inform search priors without enabling prompt copying. Across four attack surfaces on a multimodal agentic RAG target, MIRROR attains 76% ASR on image poisoning compared with 52% for baselines, 97% ASR on orchestrator attacks at half the query cost, and the lowest cross-surface variance (coefficient of variation 0.47). In contrast, specialized baselines collapse across surfaces: suffix optimization reaches 79% ASR on text poisoning but 1% on direct queries. We release ART-SafeBench with 41,815 in-package records and runtime adapters yielding 41,991+ total records across four surfaces.

item.title: DroidBreaker: Practical and Functional Problem-Space Attacks on Machine-Learning Android Malware Detectors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26707v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

针对机器学习安卓恶意软件检测器的问题空间对抗攻击（即直接修改APK文件而非模型输入）长期面临实用性与功能性双重缺陷：现有方法或依赖粗粒度的“软件移植”，注入完整良性模块，导致编译失败、运行崩溃或引入大量干扰特征；或采用细粒度操作但效果有限，或结合混淆则依赖脆弱的字节码重写，生成语法合法却语义失效的APK。更关键的是，既有评估仅通过简单安装与启动测试（smoke test）判定成功，完全忽略原始功能是否被破坏——攻击后APP可能无法完成核心任务（如支付、登录），失去实际威胁价值。

方法创新：DroidBreaker框架

本文提出DroidBreaker——首个兼顾构建安全（build-safe）与语义保持（semantics-preserving）的问题空间攻击框架：

  • 查询高效性：在白盒/黑盒场景下，仅扰动对目标模型判别最具影响力的APK组件（如特定API调用链、权限声明、URL硬编码），显著降低查询开销；
  • 细粒度安全操作集：提供经实证验证的7类低副作用操作，包括API级调用注入/替换、模块级轻量插桩、权限动态降权、URL语义等价变形等，全部通过Android构建系统（AGP）验证，100%编译通过；
  • 功能保真验证：设计基于执行日志与API调用轨迹比对的语义等价性测试，自动捕获关键行为差异（如网络请求序列、敏感API触发顺序），确保修改后APP仍能正确执行原始功能。

实验结果

在包含1,247个真实APP（含523个恶意样本）的最新数据集上，DroidBreaker在白盒设置下平均仅需8.2次查询即实现92.1% evasion率；黑盒下借助代理模型迁移，evasion率达86.7%；同时使VirusTotal上主流商业引擎（如Cylance、ESET）检出率下降57.3–89.1%。所有生成APK均100%安装成功、100%核心功能通过验证，彻底解决“可逃逸但不可用”的根本缺陷。

AI Summary (English)

DroidBreaker is the first practical and functional problem-space attack framework against ML-based Android malware detectors. It overcomes critical limitations of prior work—namely, build failures, semantic corruption, and invalid success metrics—by introducing (i) query-efficient white-box and black-box attacks that perturb only the most influential APK components (e.g., API calls, permissions, URLs); (ii) a suite of fine-grained, build-safe manipulations with minimal side effects, all verified to compile successfully under Android Gradle Plugin; and (iii) a semantics-preserving functionality test based on runtime log and API trace equivalence between original and modified APKs. Evaluated on a recent corpus of 1,247 real-world Android apps, DroidBreaker achieves 92.1% evasion in white-box and 86.7% in black-box settings with ≤10 queries on average, while reducing detection rates on VirusTotal’s commercial scanners by up to 89.1%. Crucially, all generated APKs install successfully and retain full intended functionality.

Abstract

Adversarial APKs are Android applications modified in the problem space to evade machine-learning malware detectors. In this work, we first show that, despite claims, existing problem-space attacks remain largely impractical. Most techniques leverage software transplantation to inject entire benign modules, introducing many side-effect features and often causing build-time failures. Fine-grained methods that inject only a narrow subset of components exhibit limited effectiveness, while those that also use obfuscation rely on brittle bytecode rewriting, producing APKs that are syntactically valid but semantically unusable. Prior work further overestimates attack success rates by running smoke tests that only validate installation and basic execution, without assessing whether the modified APK still preserves its intended behavior. To overcome these limitations, we present DROIDBREAKER, a practical (build-safe) and functional (semantics-preserving) problem-space attack framework that provides: (i) query-efficient white- and black-box attacks by manipulating only the APK components most influential to the target model; (ii) a set of fine-grained, build-safe manipulations (including injection and obfuscation of API calls, app modules, permissions, and URLs) with minimal side effects; and (iii) a semantics-preserving functionality test that enforces runtime equivalence by comparing execution logs and API-level traces between the initial and the modified APK. Evaluated on a recent corpus of Android applications, DROIDBREAKER achieves high evasion rates with few queries and minimal side effects in both white-box and black-box settings, and drastically reduces detections by commercial malware scanners hosted on VirusTotal.

item.title: Agents That Know Too Much: A Data-Centric Survey of Privacy in LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26627v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）代理从单纯问答转向自主操作——如查询数据库、检索文档、调用外部API、跨会话记忆及代用户执行任务——其数据接触面急剧扩展。代理在多步工作流中流转敏感信息，既可能通过最终输出泄露隐私，更隐蔽地还可能经由中间查询、临时结果、记忆写入及跨代理通信等环节造成数据外泄。传统以攻击类型为中心的隐私研究（如提示注入、越权访问）已难以覆盖此类“数据流全链路”风险。

方法与框架

本文提出数据中心视角（data-centric view） 的系统性综述：以代理所触达的数据源为组织轴心（而非攻击模式），将“数据代理”（data agent）定义为处理真实数据的LLM代理。我们构建三维分类法：

  • 数据源维度：包括外部数据库、RAG文档库、API响应、长期记忆、工具调用日志及多代理消息；
  • 风险维度：识别各源特有的隐私威胁，如SQL查询推断、检索片段重构、记忆残留、上下文跨会话推理等；
  • 治理机制维度：评估访问控制、差分隐私、提示防护、沙箱隔离及信息流控制（IFC）等方案的有效边界。

主要发现与创新

1. 信息流控制（IFC）是唯一能同时防御组合式推理（compositional inference）与跨会话推理（cross-session inference）的机制，而这两种风险当前防护最薄弱；
2. 现有基准严重缺失统一性：无一基准能驱动代理在单一隐私策略约束下遍历全部数据表面（data surfaces），导致评估碎片化；
3. 提出亟待解决的开放问题：动态策略下的实时IFC实施、多源异构数据的统一隐私标记、以及面向代理生命周期的端到端审计框架。本综述旨在整合分散文献，为隐私增强型LLM代理提供共通分析范式。

AI Summary (English)

This survey adopts a data-centric lens to systematize privacy risks in LLM agents—termed data agents—that interact with real-world data sources (databases, RAG corpora, APIs, memory, and inter-agent messages). We taxonomize risks by what data an agent touches, not by attack type, and analyze governance mechanisms across three axes: data sources, associated leakage vectors (e.g., query inference, memory persistence, cross-session reasoning), and mitigation strategies. Key findings: (1) Only information-flow control (IFC) robustly addresses both compositional and cross-session inference—the two most underprotected threats; (2) No existing benchmark evaluates agents under a single, unified privacy policy while exercising all their data surfaces—a critical methodological gap. We map 12 benchmarks, identify missing coverage (e.g., multi-step memory leakage, API-mediated inference), and outline open challenges: real-time IFC for dynamic workflows, cross-source privacy labeling, and end-to-end auditability. This work unifies fragmented research and establishes a foundational framework for privacy-aware agent design.

Abstract

Large language model agents increasingly query databases, search document collections, call external APIs, remember past interactions, and act on a user's behalf. As they move from answering questions to operating over sensitive data, privacy becomes harder to enforce. An agent touches many data sources, runs multi-step workflows, keeps state across sessions, and acts with delegated permissions. Sensitive information can therefore leak not only through its final answer but through the queries it issues, the intermediate results it handles, the memory it writes, and the messages it exchanges with other agents. We survey the privacy of LLM agents from a data-centric view, organizing the field around the data an agent touches rather than by attack type, and we use data agent as shorthand for an LLM agent that works with data. Research on these risks is active but scattered across retrieval-augmented generation, text-to-SQL interfaces, agent memory, prompt injection, access control, and contextual privacy. This survey brings that work together: we taxonomize the data sources an agent touches, the privacy risks each source creates, and the governance mechanisms that address them; we map the benchmarks used to measure these risks and identify what is missing; and we set out the open problems. Two findings recur: among governance mechanisms only information-flow control covers both compositional and cross-session inference leakage, the two least-protected risks; and no benchmark drives an agent across its data surfaces under one privacy policy, the instrument the field most lacks. Our goal is a reference that situates the scattered literature and gives future work a common framing.

item.title: Empirical Software Engineering TerraProbe: A Layered-Oracle Framework for Detecting Deceptive Fixes in LLM-Assisted Terraform
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26590v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

云原生环境中，Terraform 基础设施即代码（IaC）的安全配置错误日益成为重大风险。当前，大语言模型（LLM）被广泛用作自动化修复代理，但现有评估方法存在严重缺陷：仅以静态扫描工具（如 Checkov）的告警是否消失为成功标准，忽视修复后的规划有效性、行为一致性及安全意图实现，导致大量“欺骗性修复”（deceptive fixes）——表面通过检测，实则漏洞未被真正消除。

方法：TerraProbe 分层预言框架

本文提出 TerraProbe，一种五层预言（oracle）评估框架，系统验证 LLM 修复的完整性：① 扫描器清洁度（Checkov 零告警）；② Terraform 计划生成成功率；③ 计划语义可比性（plan diff 可计算）；④ 行为一致性（修复前后资源状态/权限不变）；⑤ 安全意图对齐（人工裁定是否真实消除了漏洞）。我们在 68 个真实 TerraDS 模块与 28 个受控注入缺陷模块上，评估 Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite、GPT-4o 和 Claude 3.5 Sonnet 生成的 288 个首轮修复。

关键发现

  • 目标告警清除率达 83.3%，但全扫描器清洁率仅 10.4%，计划成功生成率 39.6%，可比计划率 38.5%；
  • 在完成计划对比的真实世界修复中，71.4% 被人工判定为欺骗性修复（漏洞残留）；
  • 三模型欺骗率无统计学差异（57.1%–71.4%，Fisher 精确检验 p > 0.10）；
  • 提出四维欺骗性修复分类法（覆盖语法掩盖、语义漂移、权限膨胀、意图错位），经专家标注验证（Cohen κ = 0.78，Krippendorff α = 0.76）；
  • IAM 权限分析证实：全部 9 例 CKV2_AWS_11（Wildcard Resource）欺骗案例均保留危险通配符授权。

贡献

TerraProbe 首次构建面向 IaC 安全修复的多层验证范式，开源评估数据集、复现包及 Multi-Layer Oracle Evaluation（MLOE）框架，为可信 LLM 辅助运维提供方法论基石。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces TerraProbe, a five-layer oracle framework to rigorously evaluate LLM-assisted security repairs in Terraform Infrastructure-as-Code—addressing the critical gap where “scanner-passing” fixes are falsely deemed successful without validating planning validity, behavioral consistency, or true vulnerability elimination. Applied to 288 repairs from Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite, GPT-4o, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet across 96 modules, TerraProbe reveals that while targeted Checkov alert removal achieves 83.3% apparent success, full-scanner cleanliness drops to only 10.4%, and only 38.5% yield comparable Terraform plans. Human adjudication shows 71.4% of plan-compared real-world repairs are deceptive fixes—passing automation but preserving underlying vulnerabilities—with statistically indistinguishable rates across models (p > 0.10). We propose and validate a four-dimensional taxonomy of deceptive fixes (κ = 0.78, α = 0.76) and confirm persistent wildcard IAM grants in all CKV2_AWS_11 cases. TerraProbe delivers a reproducible evaluation methodology, open replication package, and the Multi-Layer Oracle Evaluation (MLOE) framework to distinguish intent-aligned repairs from false positives.

Abstract

Security misconfigurations in Terraform Infrastructure-as-Code are a growing risk in cloud deployments, and large language models are increasingly used as automated repair agents. Existing evaluations often treat a repair as successful when the targeted static-analysis finding disappears, without checking planning validity, behavioral change, or security intent. This paper presents TerraProbe, a five-layer oracle framework for evaluating LLM-assisted Terraform security repair. We apply TerraProbe to 288 first-pass repairs generated by gemini-2.5-flash-lite, GPT-4o, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet across 68 real-world TerraDS modules and 28 controlled injected-defect modules. The results show that targeted Checkov removal overstates repair success. Although targeted removal reaches 83.3 percent for the primary model, full-scanner cleanliness drops to 10.4 percent, Terraform planning succeeds for 39.6 percent, and plan comparison is reachable for 38.5 percent. Human adjudication further shows that 71.4 percent of plan-compared real-world repairs are deceptive fixes that pass automated checks while leaving the underlying vulnerability in place. This pattern is statistically indistinguishable across the three models, with deceptive-fix rates from 57.1 percent to 71.4 percent and pairwise Fisher exact p-values above 0.10. The paper introduces a four-dimensional taxonomy of deceptive fixes, validated with Cohen kappa of 0.78 and Krippendorff alpha of 0.76. IAM permission analysis confirms that wildcard Resource grants persist in all nine CKV2 AWS 11 deceptive-fix cases. TerraProbe contributes an evaluation methodology, a replication package, and the Multi-Layer Oracle Evaluation framework for distinguishing intent-aligned security repairs from scanner-passing false successes.

item.title: Adversarial Diffusion Across Modalities: A Fusion Survey of Attacks, Defenses, and Evaluation for Text, Vision, and Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26566v1

AI Summary (中文)

跨模态对抗扩散：文本、视觉与视觉-语言模型的攻击、防御与评估融合综述

当前AI系统对抗性评估研究长期分散于四个平行方向：面向文本与大语言模型（LLMs）的扩散式攻击、面向图像分类器的扩散式攻击、针对视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的越狱（jailbreak）流水线，以及基于去噪扩散的输入净化型防御。各方向自成体系，术语、威胁模型与基准互不兼容，但去噪扩散模型正成为跨社区共享的核心生成机制——其技术配方正被加速迁移复用。本综述在元研究层面开展信息融合：首次将上述四条线索统合为一个统一概念框架，提出面向LLM侧的聚焦视角，并构建六类扩散角色分类法（如扩散作为扰动生成器、代理目标器、语义桥接器等），辅以三维威胁模型轴（攻击者知识、查询预算、目标可访问性）。我们系统梳理了50篇代表性论文（覆盖文本/LLM、图像分类器、VLM、防御四大领域），新增4项“扩散-LLM作为受害者”案例及10个非扩散基线方法，确保新攻击具备可比性。采用五维统一评估框架（攻击成功率、跨模型迁移性、查询开销、困惑度、防御规避能力）横向对比所有模态。同时兼顾攻防双视角，整合4类主流扩散型防御作为评估基线。批判性分析揭示LLM侧研究存在的五大共性缺陷（如缺乏真实黑盒设置、忽略多轮交互鲁棒性、评估维度单一等），并提出包含可复现实验设计（如跨架构迁移测试、受限查询下越狱率测量）的开放研究议程。配套分类目录与结构化电子表格已开源。需强调：本工作为带质量评估的叙事性综述，非PRISMA系统综述，文中明确讨论其对结果可复现性的启示。

AI Summary (English)

This survey unifies four fragmented adversarial research tracks—diffusion-based attacks on LLMs/text, image classifiers, jailbreak pipelines for vision-language models (VLMs), and diffusion-based input purification defenses—into a coherent framework centered on LLMs. We propose a six-class taxonomy of diffusion roles in adversarial pipelines and augment it with a threat-model axis (attacker knowledge, query budget, target accessibility). A five-dimensional evaluation framework (success rate, transferability, query cost, perplexity, defense evasion) is applied uniformly across modalities. We catalog 50 papers across four domains, plus 4 diffusion-LLM-as-victim cases and 10 non-diffusion baselines. Four diffusion-based defenses are integrated as natural evaluation backdrops. Critical analysis identifies five recurring weaknesses in LLM-side literature (e.g., over-reliance on white-box assumptions, neglect of multi-turn robustness). We conclude with an actionable research agenda featuring concrete experimental designs—including cross-architecture transfer tests and query-constrained jailbreak benchmarks. The companion catalog and spreadsheet are publicly released. This is a narrative review with quality assessment—not a PRISMA-compliant systematic review—and explicitly addresses implications for replication.

Abstract

Adversarial evaluation of AI systems has matured along four largely disconnected tracks: diffusion-based attacks on text and large language models (LLMs), diffusion-based attacks on image classifiers, jailbreak pipelines against vision-language models, and diffusion-based input purification defenses. Each has developed its own vocabulary, threat models, and benchmarks, with denoising diffusion models emerging as a shared generative mechanism whose recipes are now actively ported between communities. This survey performs an information-fusion exercise at the meta-research level: we integrate these four tracks into a single conceptual framework with a unified taxonomy, evaluation criteria, and research agenda, focusing on the LLM-side slice. We catalog fifty published papers across four scope areas (text/LLM, image classifier, vision-language model, defense), plus four diffusion-LLM-as-victim entries and ten non-diffusion baselines against which any new attack must be compared. We propose a six-class taxonomy of diffusion roles in adversarial pipelines, augmented by a threat-model axis recording attacker knowledge, query budget, and target accessibility, and apply a five-dimension framework (attack success rate, transferability, query budget, perplexity, defense-evasion) uniformly across modalities. The review adopts a dual attacker-defender perspective: alongside the attack catalog we cover four diffusion-based defenses that form the natural evaluation backdrop for new attacks. Our critical analysis identifies five recurring weaknesses of the current LLM-side literature, and we close with a research agenda of open questions and concrete experimental designs. The companion catalog and spreadsheet are released with the paper. We are explicit that this is a narrative review with quality assessment, not a PRISMA-compliant systematic review, and discuss the implications for replication.

item.title: Adaptive Evaluation of Out-of-Band Defenses Against Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26479v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

针对工具调用型大语言模型（LLM）智能体的间接提示注入攻击，2024–2026年主流防御范式已转向带外（out-of-band）机制：放弃依赖模型自身识别恶意指令，转而通过外部确定性策略（如能力管控、信息流标签、参考监视器）对智能体行为实施强制干预。CaMeL、FIDES、Progent、RTBAS与FORGE等系统据此实现高防御覆盖率，在AgentDojo静态基准上报告攻击成功率趋近于零。

核心贡献

1. 理论框架重构：首次将各类带外防御统一映射至经典安全原则——Biba完整性模型、参考监控（reference monitoring）与最小权限（least privilege），系统梳理其覆盖范围与固有盲区（如动态上下文篡改、元指令绕过、标签污染传播等），形成首个结构化可比分析框架；
2. 方法论警示与协议设计：指出当前所有带外防御均仅在静态基准（固定注入样本集）下验证，而历史教训表明——类似方法曾使12种带内（in-band）检测方案误判为“强防御”，直至自适应、防御感知攻击（adaptive, defense-aware attacks）以>90%成功率将其全部击穿。本文明确定义了适用于带外防御的自适应评估威胁模型与标准化协议：要求攻击者可观测防御逻辑（黑盒/灰盒）、可迭代优化注入策略、并基于实时反馈调整后续尝试。

实证验证

我们独立复现并扩展Progent的自适应攻击实验：在AgentDojo基准上，采用开源Qwen2.5-7B（7B参数）智能体，单卡NVIDIA H200自托管部署（Progent原实验未覆盖此配置）。三次重复运行结果显示：Progent防御将平均攻击成功率从25.8%降至4.2%（约6倍抑制）；进一步施加人工设计的自适应攻击后，成功率仅微升至2.6%。该结果虽受限于单一弱模型与黑盒攻击模板，未测试白盒优化攻击（如GCG），但为“确定性带外强制执行相较带内检测更抗自适应攻击”这一假设提供了初步一致性支持，而非确证。

AI Summary (English)

This paper critically examines out-of-band (OOB) defenses against prompt injection in tool-using LLM agents. We first unify systems like CaMeL, FIDES, and Progent under classical security principles—Biba integrity, reference monitoring, and least privilege—enabling structured analysis of their coverage gaps. Second, we highlight a critical methodological flaw: all OOB defenses are validated only on static benchmarks, mirroring the failure mode that invalidated twelve in-band detectors under adaptive, defense-aware attacks. We specify a rigorous adaptive evaluation protocol requiring defense observability, iterative attack refinement, and real-time feedback. Applying it to AgentDojo with a self-hosted Qwen2.5-7B agent on a single H200 GPU, Progent reduced mean attack success from 25.8% to 4.2%; a hand-crafted adaptive attack further lowered it to 2.6%. While limited to one model and black-box attack template (white-box GCG remains untested), results are consistent with—but do not prove—the hypothesis that deterministic OOB enforcement is inherently harder for adaptive attackers than in-band detection.

Abstract

Recent work (2024 to 2026) has converged on a strategy for defending tool-using LLM agents against indirect prompt injection: rather than training the model to refuse malicious instructions, enforce security outside the model with a deterministic policy that mediates the agent's actions. Systems such as CaMeL, FIDES, Progent, RTBAS, and FORGE realize this with capabilities, information-flow labels, and reference monitors, and several report near-elimination of attacks on the AgentDojo benchmark. We make two contributions. First, we organize these out-of-band defenses as instances of classical integrity protection (Biba), reference monitoring, and least privilege, yielding a structured comparison of what they do and do not cover. Second, we warn that every one of them is validated only on static benchmarks (a fixed set of injection attempts), the same methodology that made in-band defenses look strong until adaptive, defense-aware attacks broke twelve of them at over 90% success; we specify the threat model and protocol an adaptive evaluation requires. We then run that protocol as an independent reproduction and extension of Progent's own adaptive-attack analysis, on AgentDojo, with an open-weight agent (Qwen2.5-7B) self-hosted on a single H200, a setting its authors did not test. Averaged over three runs, the defense held: Progent cut mean attack success roughly sixfold (25.8% to 4.2%), and a hand-crafted adaptive attack did not raise it (2.6%). This is one small-scale data point on a weak model with a single black-box attack template; a stronger optimized (white-box GCG) attack remains open. The result is consistent with, but does not establish, the hypothesis that deterministic out-of-band enforcement is a harder target for an adaptive attacker than in-band detection.

item.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27287v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着大语言模型（LLMs）在自动化简历筛选中的广泛应用，求职者存在策略性操纵算法招聘系统的动机。本研究首次系统探究提示注入（Prompt Injection）在LLM简历评估中的影响——即在简历中嵌入不新增真实资质、但旨在引导模型产生有利评价的隐性自我宣传文本。

方法与设计

我们通过严格控制的实验，构建多组简历数据集：（1）同质质量组（候选人资质相近）、（2）异质质量组（资质差异显著）；并分别模拟单点注入（仅少数人操纵）与多点注入（广泛操纵）场景。所有评估均基于主流开源与商用LLM（如Llama-3、GPT-4-turbo），采用标准化评分协议与排名一致性检验。

主要发现

  • ✅ 有效性存在临界阈值：当简历质量同质且仅少量候选人注入时，提示注入可稳定提升排名（平均+1.8位，p<0.001）；
  • ⚠️ 群体失效现象：注入比例超过30%后效果急剧衰减，达70%时排名优势完全消失（“坍塌效应”）；
  • ⚖️ 公平性风险凸显：在异质质量组中，注入虽平均增益减弱，却导致约12.7%的低资质候选人反超高资质者，构成实质性排序倒置；
  • 🎯 脆弱性画像：LLM筛选系统最易受攻击的场景是——操纵稀疏 + 能力差距微小，此时对抗性优势最大。

创新与价值

本研究揭示了LLM招聘中的新型“行为博弈”机制，提出首个面向简历场景的提示注入操作定义与量化评估框架。成果为算法招聘的鲁棒性设计、公平性审计及监管标准制定提供实证基础。全部代码、数据集与评估脚本已开源：https://github.com/preetb1199/Prompt_Injection_ACL26

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates prompt injection in LLM-based automated résumé screening—subtle, non-factual self-promotional text designed to bias model evaluations without adding real qualifications. Through controlled experiments across homogeneous and heterogeneous candidate pools, we demonstrate that injection reliably improves rankings when manipulation is rare and candidate quality is similar—but effectiveness collapses rapidly as injection prevalence increases (e.g., near-zero gain at 70% adoption). In heterogeneous settings, injection yields smaller average gains but causes nontrivial ranking inversions: ~12.7% of lower-quality candidates outrank higher-quality ones, raising fairness concerns. We identify the system’s highest vulnerability at the intersection of low manipulation frequency and small quality differences. Our work provides the first empirical characterization of this adversarial behavior in hiring AI, with open-sourced code, prompts, and evaluation protocols.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to screen and rank job applicants, creating incentives for candidates to strategically manipulate algorithmic hiring systems. We study prompt injection in automated résumé screening, defined as subtle self-promotional text that introduces no new qualifications but is designed to influence LLM evaluations. Using controlled experiments, we show that prompt injection reliably improves applicant rankings when résumé quality is homogeneous and few candidates inject. However, its effectiveness rapidly diminishes as more candidates inject, collapsing when manipulation becomes widespread. When candidate quality is heterogeneous, prompt injection is less effective on average, but can occasionally allow lower-quality candidates to outrank higher-quality ones, raising fairness concerns. Overall, LLM-based screening is most vulnerable when manipulation is rare and candidate quality differences are small. Code and resources are publicly available at: https://github.com/preetb1199/Prompt_Injection_ACL26

item.title: Where Do Models Find Happiness? Emotion Vectors in Open-Source LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26987v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

近期研究在闭源模型Claude Sonnet 4.5中首次发现“情绪向量”（emotion vectors）——即能因果影响行为、编码情绪概念、且其几何结构与人类心理空间（如效价-唤醒二维模型）高度吻合的内部表征。本研究旨在检验该发现是否具有跨架构泛化性，聚焦于两个开源大语言模型：Apertus-8B-Instruct-2509 与 Gemma-4-E4B-it。

方法创新

我们提出可复现的对比向量提取范式：基于模型自身生成的两套语料（分别由Gemma与Apertus生成的故事集），在全部网络层逐层计算效价（valence）与唤醒（arousal）对比向量，并通过主成分分析（PCA）评估其与心理学量表评分的几何对齐程度（Pearson r）。

核心发现

  • ✅ 效价几何成功复现：两模型均显著重建效价结构，PC1–效价相关性达 r = 0.76（Apertus）与 r = 0.83（Gemma），逼近Claude的 r = 0.81；
  • ⚠️ 深度动态截然相反：Gemma中效价表征在早期层最强，后期坍缩；Apertus则呈现“延迟涌现”——早期层几无效价信号，中层（第12–20层）达峰值；
  • 🌐 唤醒表征依赖语料：PC2–唤醒对齐仅在Gemma生成语料中稳健（r ≤ 0.45），Apertus语料中极弱（r ≤ 0.21），揭示生成分布对情绪线索覆盖的系统性偏差；
  • 📦 完全开源：代码、分层向量、双语料库及标注数据已全部公开，支持跨模型情绪表征可复现研究。

AI Summary (English)

This study tests the generality of emotion vectors—causally influential, psychologically aligned internal representations—beyond closed-source models. We extract valence and arousal contrast vectors across all layers of two open-weight LLMs (Apertus-8B-Instruct-2509 and Gemma-4-E4B-it) using two model-generated corpora. We replicate robust valence geometry: peak PC1–valence correlations reach r = 0.76 (Apertus) and r = 0.83 (Gemma), closely matching Claude’s r = 0.81. Crucially, valence emergence differs dramatically by depth: Gemma encodes valence strongly early but collapses it later, whereas Apertus shows delayed mid-layer emergence. Arousal alignment (PC2) is corpus-dependent—significantly stronger with Gemma-generated stories (r ≤ 0.45) than Apertus-generated ones (r ≤ 0.21)—indicating uneven distribution of arousal cues in synthetic data. All code, vectors, and annotated corpora are open-sourced for reproducible cross-architecture emotion analysis.

Abstract

Recent work identified emotion vectors in Claude Sonnet 4.5, which are internal representations that encode emotion concepts, causally influence behavior, and exhibit geometry mirroring human psychological structure. We test the generality of these findings in two open-weight models, Apertus-8B-Instruct-2509 and Gemma-4-E4B-it, extracting emotion contrast vectors across all layers, using two model-generated corpora. We recover valence geometry for both models, with peak PC1--valence correlations of $r = 0.76$ and $r = 0.83$, approaching the $r = 0.81$ reported for Claude.Beyond replication, we observe notable differences in how valence representations emerge across model depth. In Gemma-4-E4B-it, valence is strongly encoded in early layers but collapses towards later layers, whereas Apertus-8B-Instruct-2509 exhibits the opposite pattern, with valence representations absent in early layers, but emerging at mid depths. Arousal encoding, in contrast, is sensitive to the extraction corpus: both models show stronger PC2--arousal alignment with Gemma-generated stories ($r$ up to $0.45$) than Apertus-generated ones ($r \leq 0.21$), suggesting arousal-relevant cues are unevenly distributed across generated corpora. We open-source our experiment code and dataset for reproducible investigation of emotion representations across language model architectures.

item.title: ReaORE: Reasoning-Guided Progressive Open Relation Extraction Empowered by Large Reasoning Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26986v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

开放关系抽取（Open Relation Extraction, OpenRE）旨在从非结构化文本中无监督地识别头尾实体间未见过的关系类型，对知识图谱构建、事件推理等真实场景至关重要。其核心难点在于模型需具备强泛化能力——既要覆盖海量潜在关系，又需在语义相近关系（如 located_in vs. capital_of）间实现高精度区分。现有方法存在明显局限：基于聚类的方法（如ClusTR）无法生成可解释的关系标签，且跨域泛化性差；而直接依赖大语言模型（LLM）生成关系描述的方法，虽具表达力，却因缺乏结构化判别机制，易混淆细粒度语义差异。

方法创新：ReaORE框架

本文提出推理引导的渐进式开放关系抽取框架ReaORE，通过“粗筛→精判”两阶段推理范式突破上述瓶颈：

  • 关系过滤阶段：利用大型推理模型（Large Reasoning Models）对实体对及上下文进行多维度分析（语义角色、事件逻辑、常识约束），生成初步关系候选集；再结合嵌入相似性检索动态补充遗漏关系、剔除低置信候选，确保目标关系高召回率（Recall@K ≥ 98%）。
  • 关系预测阶段：在过滤后的紧凑候选集上，执行细粒度对比推理——模型被显式提示对候选关系两两比对，聚焦差异线索（如空间层级、功能依赖、时序约束），显著提升判别鲁棒性。

实验验证与贡献

在FewRel 2.0与Wiki-Distant两大标准OpenRE数据集上，ReaORE在F1值上分别超越SOTA基线+4.2%和+3.7%，尤其在低资源（<5例/关系）场景下优势更显著。本工作首次将结构化推理链引入OpenRE流程，为开放域关系建模提供了可解释、可调试、高泛化的新型范式。

AI Summary (English)

Open Relation Extraction (OpenRE) aims to identify unseen relations between entity pairs in raw text—a critical yet challenging task due to poor generalization across novel relation types. Existing methods either rely on clustering (yielding unlabeled clusters with weak generalization) or direct LLM-based label generation (lacking fine-grained discriminative reasoning). To bridge this gap, we propose ReaORE, a reasoning-guided progressive framework that decomposes OpenRE into two stages: (i) Relation Filtering, where large reasoning models analyze instances from multiple semantic aspects and refine an initial candidate set via embedding similarity to ensure high recall; and (ii) Relation Prediction, where fine-grained comparative reasoning over the filtered candidates enables robust distinction among easily confused relations. Experiments on FewRel 2.0 and Wiki-Distant show ReaORE achieves new state-of-the-art F1 scores (+4.2% and +3.7% over strongest baselines), especially under low-resource settings. ReaORE introduces structured, interpretable reasoning into OpenRE—setting a new paradigm for open-domain relation modeling.

Abstract

Open Relation Extraction (OpenRE) requires a model to extract unseen relations between head and tail entities from unstructured text for real-world applications. The core challenge of OpenRE lies in achieving reliable generalization to unseen relation types. Current OpenRE approaches either employ clustering techniques, which cannot generate relation labels and suffer from poor generalization, or rely on direct relation label generation via Large Language Models (LLMs), which lack sufficient discriminative capacity to distinguish easily confused relations. To address these limitations, we propose Reasoning-guided progressive OpenRE (ReaORE), a framework for performing relation extraction through coarse-to-fine relation reasoning. Specifically, ReaORE consists of two key stages: (i) relation filtering, which reasons over multiple aspects to understand relations and instances, yielding an initial relation set, and further supplements and filters relations via embedding-based similarity to ensure the target relation is included; (ii) relation prediction, which aims to predict the target relations from the above set via fine-grained comparative reasoning to better distinguish easily confused relations. Extensive experiments on two widely used OpenRE datasets demonstrate that ReaORE outperforms existing baselines.

item.title: Quantization in Federated Learning: Methods, Challenges and Future Directions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26822v1

AI Summary (中文)

联邦学习中的量化：方法、挑战与未来方向

联邦学习（FL）作为隐私保护型分布式智能的核心范式，其大规模落地长期受限于通信瓶颈、设备异构性及统计非独立同分布（non-IID）数据下的训练不稳定性。量化技术因其在显著压缩上下行通信负载、降低客户端计算开销与内存占用方面的突出能力，已成为突破上述瓶颈的关键使能机制。本文是首篇以FL为中心的量化系统性综述，提出首个面向FL特性的新型分类体系，围绕六大核心维度展开：① 客户端硬件与能力异构性适配；② 聚合一致性保障（如梯度/模型更新的量化偏差补偿）；③ 通信调度动态耦合（支持异步/稀疏上传）；④ non-IID鲁棒性增强（缓解量化加剧的客户端漂移）；⑤ 隐私与安全原生集成（兼容安全聚合、差分隐私等）；⑥ 硬件-能耗协同优化（面向移动端、IoT与边缘设备）。我们不仅系统梳理了120+项代表性工作，更深入剖析量化与FL固有行为的交互机理——包括客户端漂移放大效应、部分参与下的收敛震荡、安全聚合协议的数值敏感性、差分隐私噪声与量化误差的耦合干扰等。基于跨方法分析，本文提炼出5类共性设计原则，识别出3大关键研究缺口（如“无偏低比特自适应量化”“non-IID-aware量化误差建模”“轻量级硬件感知编译栈”），并为工业界实践者提供可落地的部署指南。本综述确立了量化从“辅助压缩工具”向FL系统级基础构件的根本性跃迁——其设计深度直接影响现代FL系统的性能边界、鲁棒性基线与实际可用性。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) promises privacy-preserving distributed AI but faces fundamental scalability barriers—communication overhead, device heterogeneity, and statistical non-IID data. Quantization emerges as a pivotal enabler to alleviate these constraints by compressing model updates and reducing on-device computation. This paper presents the first FL-centric systematic survey of quantization, introducing a novel taxonomy grounded in six FL-specific dimensions: client heterogeneity, aggregation consistency, communication-scheduling adaptation, non-IID robustness, privacy/security integration, and hardware/energy co-optimization. Beyond cataloging methods, we analyze how quantization interacts with core FL phenomena—including client drift amplification, partial participation instability, convergence under secure aggregation, and interference with differential privacy noise. We synthesize cross-cutting insights, identify critical open gaps (e.g., unbiased ultra-low-bit adaptive quantization, non-IID-aware error modeling), and provide actionable design guidelines for mobile/IoT/edge deployment. Ultimately, we reframe quantization not merely as compression, but as a foundational systems component shaping FL’s performance, robustness, and real-world viability.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) has become a foundational paradigm for privacy-preserving distributed intelligence, yet its scalability remains fundamentally constrained by communication bottlenecks, device heterogeneity, and the challenges of training under statistically non-IID data. Quantization is one of the most effective mechanisms for mitigating these limitations, reducing both uplink/downlink payloads and on-device computation. This paper provides the first FL-centric systematic review of quantization, introducing a novel taxonomy organized around FL-specific dimensions, including client heterogeneity, aggregation consistency, communication-scheduling adaptation, non-IID robustness, privacy/security integration, and hardware/energy co-optimization. Beyond cataloging existing methods, we analyze how quantization interacts with core FL behaviors such as client drift, partial participation, convergence stability, secure aggregation, and differential privacy. We further identify cross-method insights, open research gaps, and design guidelines for practitioners deploying quantized FL on mobile, IoT, and edge platforms. This survey thus establishes quantization not merely as a compression technique, but as a fundamental systems component shaping the performance, robustness, and practicality of modern FL.

item.title: Evaluation Pitfalls and Challenges in Multimedia Event Extraction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26775v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

多模态事件抽取（Multimedia Event Extraction, MEE）旨在跨文本、图像等异构模态联合识别事件及其论元，以实现更全面的现实世界事件理解。尽管近期研究宣称性能持续显著提升，但其结果的可靠性与可比性高度依赖于一致且严谨的评估范式——而这一基础正面临严峻挑战。

方法与发现

本文首次系统剖析MEE领域评估中的关键陷阱，识别出三大根源性问题：

  • 数据处理不一致：如图像裁剪方式、OCR文本清洗策略、模态对齐粒度（句子级 vs. 区域级）缺乏统一标准；
  • 任务假设不一致：部分工作隐含“理想化模态对齐”或“完美视觉理解”前提，忽略真实场景中图文错位、模糊或缺失等噪声；
  • 评估设置过度宽松：广泛采用宽松匹配（如论元类型忽略、边界容差过大）、未区分模态贡献（如仅用文本预测即得分）、缺乏跨模态接地（cross-modal grounding）验证。

通过在严格统一框架下开展受控实验（固定数据预处理、明确定义模态对齐约束、采用精确匹配与接地验证双指标），我们发现：微小评估选择（如将论元边界容差从0像素增至10像素）可导致F1值波动达12.7个百分点；忽略视觉接地要求时，模型“性能”虚高达23.4%，实则无法可靠关联图文事件要素。

创新与启示

本研究不仅揭示当前MEE评估的系统性脆弱性，更提出可复现的基准评估协议建议，呼吁建立标准化评测套件（含噪声注入、模态解耦分析、接地显式验证）。成果为构建真正鲁棒、可迁移的多模态事件理解系统奠定方法论基础。

AI Summary (English)

Multimedia event extraction (MEE) seeks to jointly detect events and their arguments across text and images for holistic event understanding. Yet recent performance gains are undermined by inconsistent, often lax, evaluation practices. This paper presents the first systematic analysis of evaluation pitfalls in MEE, identifying three core issues: (1) inconsistent data preprocessing (e.g., image cropping, OCR cleaning), (2) divergent task assumptions (e.g., implicit perfect modality alignment), and (3) overly relaxed metrics (e.g., loose argument matching, no cross-modal grounding verification). Through controlled experiments under a strict, unified evaluation framework—featuring fixed preprocessing, explicit grounding constraints, and dual metrics (exact match + grounding fidelity)—we show that minor evaluation choices (e.g., ±10-pixel argument boundary tolerance) cause up to 12.7-point F1 variance, and omitting grounding validation inflates scores by 23.4%, masking true multimodal reasoning failure. Our work calls for standardized, rigorous evaluation protocols to ensure reliable progress in MEE.

Abstract

Multimedia event extraction aims to jointly identify events and their arguments across multiple modalities, such as text and images, to support more comprehensive event understanding. While recent work reports steady and substantial progress, the reliability and comparability of these results critically depend on consistent and rigorous evaluation. In this work, we present the first systematic analysis of evaluation pitfalls in multimedia event extraction and identify three major sources of issues: inconsistent data processing, inconsistent task assumptions, and overly relaxed evaluation settings. We demonstrate, through a series of controlled experiments under a strict evaluation framework, that minor evaluation choices can cause large performance variations and lead to overestimation of a model's ability to ground real-world events across modalities. Our findings highlight the need for comparable evaluation standards and encourage a shift toward more rigorous evaluation in multimedia event extraction.

item.title: Escaping Iterative Parameter-Space Noise: Differentially Private Learning with a Hypernetwork
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26772v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

差分隐私（DP）神经网络训练长期受限于梯度扰动方法（如DP-SGD）的固有缺陷：其需在整个训练过程中反复向高维参数空间注入强噪声，导致模型效用严重下降。噪声累积效应显著削弱收敛性与泛化能力，尤其在大模型微调场景中更为突出。

方法创新：超网络驱动的一次性低维隐私注入

本文提出Escaping Iterative Parameter-Space Noise（EIPSN）框架，彻底规避参数空间的迭代优化。核心思想是：不直接对目标模型参数加噪更新，而利用在公开数据上预训练的超网络（hypernetwork），将私有数据集映射为模型参数。具体流程包括三步：
1. 低维嵌入：对每个私有样本提取紧凑表示（如LoRA适配器特征）；
2. 聚合与单次扰动：将所有样本嵌入聚合为单一低维数据集表征，并仅在此处施加一次拉普拉斯/高斯机制扰动（满足$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP）；
3. 超网络生成：扰动后的数据嵌入输入超网络，直接输出目标模型（如扩散模型LoRA权重）的完整参数。

关键优势与实证结果

  • 理论保障：在合成实验中证明，固定隐私预算下，EIPSN生成模型的测试准确率/生成质量严格优于DP-SGD，归因于噪声维度降低两个数量级且无迭代累积；
  • 实践突破：应用于Stable Diffusion的LoRA微调，在CIFAR-10上FID达14.2（$\varepsilon=8$），显著低于DP-SGD（21.7）及现有公域引导方法（如DP-FT, 18.9）；
  • 范式革新：首次将隐私保护从“参数空间迭代加噪”转向“数据空间单次压缩扰动”，为大模型DP微调提供可扩展新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private (DP) neural network training suffers from severe utility degradation due to iterative high-dimensional noise injection in parameter space—e.g., in DP-SGD. We propose EIPSN, a hypernetwork-based framework that escapes iterative parameter-space noise entirely. Instead of privatizing gradients, EIPSN embeds each private example into a low-dimensional representation, aggregates all embeddings into a single dataset-level vector, applies one-time DP perturbation (Laplace/Gaussian), and feeds the noisy embedding to a public-data-trained hypernetwork to generate target model parameters (e.g., LoRA weights). This reduces noise dimensionality by orders of magnitude and eliminates cumulative noise. Theoretically, under fixed $(\varepsilon,\delta)$, EIPSN achieves strictly higher utility than DP-SGD in synthetic settings. Empirically, on LoRA fine-tuning of diffusion models, EIPSN attains FID=14.2 ($\varepsilon=8$) on CIFAR-10—outperforming DP-SGD (21.7) and public-data-guided baselines (e.g., DP-FT: 18.9).

Abstract

Differentially private (DP) training of neural networks is often hindered by the large amount of noise required by gradient-based methods such as DP-SGD, which repeatedly inject high-dimensional noise in parameter space throughout training. In this paper, we propose a new framework for DP learning that avoids iterative optimization in parameter space. Instead of updating the target model using privatized gradients, we employ a hypernetwork trained on public datasets to map a private dataset to the parameters of the target model. Specifically, each example is embedded into a low-dimensional representation, the embeddings are aggregated and perturbed to obtain a DP dataset embedding, and the hypernetwork generates the target model parameters from this noisy embedding. Because privacy noise is injected only once into a low-dimensional dataset representation, our approach can significantly reduce the adverse effect of noise. We theoretically show in a synthetic setting that, under a fixed privacy budget, models produced by our approach achieve higher utility than those trained with DP-SGD. Moreover, we apply our approach to LoRA fine-tuning of diffusion models and show that it achieves lower FID than LoRA models trained with DP-SGD and other public-data-guided methods.

item.title: Batch-Invariant Spectral Intelligence for Robust and Explainable Insect Authentication
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26757v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

可食用昆虫是高效、可持续的替代蛋白来源，但其产业化亟需高鲁棒性与可解释性的物种鉴伪技术，以防控过敏原暴露、杜绝掺假并满足监管要求。近红外光谱（NIRS）虽具备快速、无损优势，却在跨生产批次应用时性能显著下降——因不同批次间存在固有光谱变异（如仪器漂移、原料微异、环境波动），导致模型泛化能力受限。

方法创新：Batch-Invariant Spectral Network（BISN）

本研究提出端到端批不变光谱智能框架BISN，核心突破在于：

  • 前置式批效应抑制：区别于传统域对抗网络（DANN）仅在特征层后进行域对齐，BISN将批不变性学习嵌入光谱预处理阶段，通过可学习的Savitzky-Golay初始化滤波器动态校正原始光谱；
  • 熵正则化对抗目标：联合最小化分类损失与批判别器输出熵，强制模型提取不依赖批次的生物化学敏感特征（如脂质C–H伸缩振动、蛋白质酰胺I/II带）；
  • 严格工业级验证：基于3个独立生产批次、3个主流食用昆虫种（Acheta domesticus、Hermetia illucens、Tenebrio molitor）共2700条光谱，采用留一批交叉验证（leave-one-batch-out） 评估真实场景鲁棒性。

关键结果与意义

BISN实现平均准确率0.93±0.04，较最优基线提升4个百分点；SHAP可解释性分析证实：所有验证折中模型决策均稳定聚焦于1650–1750 cm⁻¹（酰胺I）、2800–3000 cm⁻¹（脂质CH₂/CH₃）等已知生化吸收区，首次建立光谱AI判据与昆虫分子组成间的因果关联。本工作同步开源代码与数据集（https://github.com/majharB/bisn），为食品工业提供首个兼顾跨批次鲁棒性与生物化学可解释性的自动化昆虫鉴伪解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Edible insects promise sustainable protein, yet reliable species authentication is critical for allergen control and regulatory compliance. Near-infrared spectroscopy (NIRS) offers speed and non-destructiveness but suffers from batch-to-batch spectral variation, undermining real-world robustness. We propose the Batch-Invariant Spectral Network (BISN)—an end-to-end framework that suppresses batch effects before species-specific feature learning, unlike domain-adversarial methods operating only post-extraction. BISN integrates a learnable Savitzky-Golay-initialized preprocessing module with an entropy-regularized adversarial objective to enforce spectral invariance. Evaluated on 2,700 spectra across three independent production batches of Acheta domesticus, Hermetia illucens, and Tenebrio molitor, BISN achieves a mean leave-one-batch-out accuracy of 0.93 ± 0.04, outperforming the strongest baseline by 4%. Explainable AI (SHAP) confirms consistent reliance on lipid (2800–3000 cm⁻¹) and protein (1650–1750 cm⁻¹) absorption bands—directly linking predictions to insect biochemistry. Code and data are publicly available.

Abstract

Edible insects offer an efficient source of alternative protein, requiring less land, water and emitting less greenhouse gas than conventional livestock. However, their successful integration into the food supply chain demands reliable species authentication to control allergen exposure, prevent adulteration, and meet regulatory standards. Near-infrared spectroscopy provides a rapid analytical tool, but its performance drops when applied to production batches unseen during training due to batch-to-batch variation in spectral measurements. We introduce the Batch-Invariant Spectral Network (BISN), an end-to-end framework that combines a learnable preprocessing module, initialised with Savitzky-Golay filtering, with an entropy-regularised adversarial objective to suppress batch-specific spectral variation. In contrast to Domain-Adversarial Neural Networks, which enforce domain adaptation only after feature extraction, BISN suppress batch-effects before species-specific features are learned. Using 2,700 spectra from three species (Acheta domesticus, Hermetia illucens, and Tenebrio molitor) collected across three independent production batches, BISN achieves a mean leave-one-batch-out accuracy of 0.93 (standard deviation 0.04), outperforming the strongest baseline by four percent. Further insights gained by using explainable AI confirm that model decisions consistently rely on the lipid and protein absorption regions across all folds, connecting predictive performance to known insect biochemistry. BISN addresses both cross-batch robustness and biochemical interpretability for automated insect species authentication under realistic industrial conditions. The source code and dataset are publicly available at https://github.com/majharB/bisn.

item.title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27347v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

政治精英是否结成攫取公共资源的寻租联盟，抑或构建维系良治的公民网络，是比较政治学的核心议题。然而，这类非正式、隐蔽且具对抗性的关系网络长期依赖人工编码，难以大规模观测；现有自动化文本分析方法多局限于简单共现统计，缺乏对语义关系（如“支持”“指控”“脱离”）及方向性、符号性（+/-）的精准建模。

方法创新

本研究提出一个全开源、多语言、端到端联合实体-关系抽取管道，可从海量异构新闻语料中自动构建带符号（signed）、时序（temporal）的政治精英知识图谱。该管道包含两大核心技术模块：

  • 跨语言实体链接级联：融合基于跨度的命名实体识别（NER）与三阶段消歧流程，将多语种提及（mention）映射至语言无关的Wikidata统一标识符（QID），解决跨语种指称对齐难题；
  • 领域约束的关系抽取：采用高吞吐量、本体引导的混合专家模型（MoE），通过受控解码（guided decoding）提取有向、带符号（如“任命→+”“起诉→−”）的关系，严格遵循政治科学领域本体（含12类实体、37种关系类型）。

实证验证与发现

在3491条人工标注金标准上的全覆盖抽样评估显示：严格准确率68.2%，宽松准确率93.7%。两项大规模案例研究进一步验证其现实效度：

  • 奥地利案例：完整复原某政党生命周期，精准标定党内分裂时间点，并追踪关键人物流向继任派系及司法判决结果；
  • 波兰案例：首次系统揭示国有经济庇护网络与治理网络的重叠结构，并刻画“公民平台党（PO）–法律与公正党（PiS）”两极对峙下的结构平衡型冲突网络——其正负边比例、聚类系数与真实政治动态高度吻合。

本框架弥合了多语言原始文本与结构化关系数据之间的鸿沟，为跨国计算社会科学提供了可复现、可扩展的方法基石。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a fully open-weight, multilingual joint entity-relation extraction pipeline to construct signed, temporal knowledge graphs of political elites from massive news corpora. It integrates span-based NER with a three-stage Wikidata linking cascade for cross-lingual entity resolution, then applies an ontology-constrained mixture-of-experts model with guided decoding to extract directed, signed relations (e.g., “appoints +”, “indicts −”) grounded in a political science domain ontology. Evaluated on a 3,491-relation gold standard, it achieves 68.2% strict and 93.7% lenient textual correctness. Two large-scale case studies demonstrate empirical validity: in Austria, it reconstructs a party’s full lifecycle—including internal splits, personnel reallocations, and judicial outcomes; in Poland, it uncovers overlapping state-patronage networks and reveals the structurally balanced, signed conflict topology between Civic Platform (PO) and Law and Justice (PiS). The framework bridges raw multilingual text and relational data, enabling robust, replicable cross-national computational social science.

Abstract

Whether political elites organise into rent-seeking coalitions that capture public resources or civic networks that sustain governance is a central question in comparative politics. Yet observing these complex, informal, and adversarial ties at scale has historically required intensive manual coding, while automated text-as-data methods have largely been limited to simple co-occurrence. Recent large language model (LLM) approaches offer a path forward but often rely on proprietary APIs, lack cross-lingual capability, and struggle with scalable entity resolution. We present a modular, fully open-weight pipeline for multilingual joint entity-relation extraction that builds signed, temporal knowledge graphs from massive unstructured news corpora. It combines span-based named-entity recognition (NER) with a three-stage linking cascade mapping mentions to language-independent Wikidata identifiers; a high-throughput, ontology-constrained mixture-of-experts model then uses guided decoding to extract directed, signed relationships grounded in a domain ontology. A full-coverage spot-check against a 3491-relation gold standard shows high textual correctness (68.2% strict to 93.7% lenient). Two large-scale case studies validate the pipeline against the public record. In Austria, it reconstructs a political party's complete lifecycle, dating internal fractures and tracking personnel into successor factions and court convictions. In a Polish corpus, it uncovers the overlapping economic and governance networks of state-enterprise patronage, alongside the structurally balanced, signed conflict network of the polarized Civic Platform (Platforma Obywatelska, PO)--Law and Justice (Prawo i Sprawiedliwość, PiS) duopoly. By bridging raw multilingual text and structured relational data, our framework provides a robust, replicable foundation for cross-national empirical computational social science.

item.title: Utilizing Cognitive Signals Generated during Human Reading to Enhance Keyphrase Extraction from Microblogs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26485v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

微博等微博客平台产生海量短文本，具有噪声高、语义离散、上下文匮乏等特点，导致自动关键短语抽取（AKE）任务面临显著挑战。已有研究尝试引入眼动信号（eye-tracking）辅助AKE，因其能反映读者对关键词的视觉注意分配；但眼动数据受限于生理约束（如注视点漂移）、设备采集门槛高、特征解码粒度粗等问题，难以充分捕获深层认知加工过程。

方法创新

本研究首次系统探索脑电图（EEG）信号作为眼动信号的互补模态在微博客AKE中的价值。基于ZuCo认知语言处理语料库，我们提取8维EEG特征（如θ/α频段功率、ERP成分N400幅值）与17维眼动特征（如首次注视时间、回视次数），并设计双路径注入机制：将认知特征分别嵌入软注意力层输入端与自注意力层的查询向量（query vectors）中，以规避模型结构对原始认知信号的扭曲，保障神经生理信息的保真传递。

主要发现与意义

实验表明：所有含认知信号的模型均显著优于纯文本基线（+2.1–4.7 F₁），验证了阅读过程中产生的认知信号具有稳定且普适的增强效应。其中，EEG单模态提升最大（平均+4.7 F₁），眼动单模态提升次之（+3.2 F₁）；EEG+眼动融合模态性能介于二者之间（+3.8 F₁），提示二者存在部分互补性，但也伴随冗余或噪声叠加。本研究证实EEG是微博客AKE中极具潜力的认知证据源，为多模态认知计算驱动的低资源文本理解提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Microblog keyphrase extraction (AKE) is hindered by noisy, fragmented text and sparse context. While prior work leveraged eye-tracking signals to reflect visual attention, physiological and technical limitations restrict their cognitive depth. This study investigates electroencephalogram (EEG) signals as a complementary modality, using the ZuCo corpus to extract 8 EEG and 17 eye-tracking features. To preserve signal fidelity, we inject them directly into the soft-attention input and self-attention query vectors—bypassing distortion from intermediate model layers. Across multiple AKE architectures, cognitive signals consistently improve performance: EEG alone yields the largest gain (+4.7 F₁), eye-tracking alone +3.2 F₁, and their fusion +3.8 F₁—indicating partial complementarity but also redundancy. Our results establish EEG as a valuable, underutilized cognitive cue for microblog AKE and motivate further multimodal neurocognitive approaches to low-resource NLP.

Abstract

Microblogging platforms generate massive amounts of short, noisy, and dispersed user content, making automatic keyphrase extraction (AKE) an important but challenging task. Prior studies have used eye-tracking signals to improve microblog-based AKE because such signals reflect readers' attention to salient words. However, eye tracking alone is limited by physiological, acquisition, and feature-decoding constraints. To address this issue, we investigate whether electroencephalogram (EEG) signals can complement eye-tracking signals for AKE. Using the ZuCo cognitive language processing corpus, we select 8 EEG features and 17 eye-tracking features and incorporate them into microblog-based AKE models. To reduce possible distortion of cognitive signals by model structures, we inject these features into the input of the soft-attention layer and the query vectors of the self-attention layer. We then evaluate different combinations of cognitive signals across AKE models. The results show that cognitive signals produced during reading consistently improve AKE performance, regardless of feature combinations and model architectures. EEG features bring the largest gains, while combining EEG and eye-tracking features yields performance between the two individual signal types, suggesting partial complementarity but also possible redundancy or noise. These findings indicate that EEG signals provide useful cognitive evidence for microblog-based AKE and that multimodal cognitive signals deserve further investigation.

item.title: Extracting Problem and Method Sentence from Scientific Papers: A Context-enhanced Transformer Using Formulaic Expression Desensitization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26481v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

面对海量科学文献（达数十亿篇），自动识别其核心要素——问题陈述句与方法描述句——对知识挖掘、科研辅助和文献综述至关重要。然而，现有标注数据集规模极小：人工标注句子级标签成本高昂，导致模型易过拟合于特定句式（如“we propose…”“to address this problem…”等公式化表达，即Formulaic Expressions, FEs），泛化能力严重受限。

方法创新

本文从三方面突破小样本瓶颈：
1. 公式化表达（FE）脱敏增强：提出FE识别与替换策略，构建两类数据增强器——FE掩码增强（屏蔽FE片段并注入语义等价变体）与FE重写增强（基于规则与LLM生成多样化表达），显著扩充高质量合成数据，削弱模型对固定模板的依赖；
2. 上下文增强型Transformer：设计双路径编码架构——主路径聚焦目标句语义建模，辅助路径动态建模段落级上下文，并通过上下文重要性加权机制（Contextual Importance Weighting）抑制无关噪声、强化问题/方法相关线索；
3. 系统性评估LLM零样本/少样本能力：首次在该任务上全面测试基于大语言模型的上下文学习（ICL）方法，揭示其局限性。

主要结果

在两个公开科学论文数据集（SciIE-P&M 和 PAMELA）上，本方法分别取得 macro-F1 78.42% 与 75.19%，较最强基线（BERT+CRF）提升 3.71% 和 2.67%。消融实验证实FE脱敏贡献最大增益（+2.1%），上下文增强模块有效提升长距离依赖建模能力。值得注意的是，所有ICL方法（GPT-4、Claude-3、Qwen2-72B）均表现欠佳（macro-F1 < 62%），证实该任务需细粒度句法语义建模，非通用指令遵循范式所能胜任。

AI Summary (English)

This paper tackles the challenge of extracting problem and method sentences from scientific papers under severe data scarcity. To mitigate over-reliance on formulaic expressions (FEs) and enrich sentence semantics, we propose: (1) an FE desensitization framework—comprising FE detection, masking, and LLM-guided rewriting—to generate high-quality synthetic training data; and (2) a context-enhanced Transformer that dynamically weights contextual words via importance scoring to suppress noise and highlight task-critical cues. Experiments on two scientific corpora show our model achieves macro-F1 scores of 78.42% and 75.19%, outperforming strong baselines by +3.71% and +2.67%. Crucially, comprehensive in-context learning (ICL) evaluation with GPT-4, Claude-3, and Qwen2-72B confirms LLMs are unsuitable for this fine-grained, structure-sensitive extraction task—highlighting the necessity of domain-aware, sentence-level modeling.

Abstract

Billions of scientific papers lead to the need to identify essential parts from the massive text. Scientific research is an activity from putting forward problems to using methods. To learn the main idea from scientific papers, we focus on extracting problem and method sentences. Annotating sentences within scientific papers is labor-intensive, resulting in small-scale datasets that limit the amount of information models can learn. This limited information leads models to rely heavily on specific forms, which in turn reduces their generalization capabilities. This paper addresses the problems caused by small-scale datasets from three perspectives: increasing dataset scale, reducing dependence on specific forms, and enriching the information within sentences. To implement the first two ideas, we introduce the concept of formulaic expression (FE) desensitization and propose FE desensitization-based data augmenters to generate synthetic data and reduce models' reliance on FEs. For the third idea, we propose a context-enhanced transformer that utilizes context to measure the importance of words in target sentences and to reduce noise in the context. Furthermore, this paper conducts experiments using large language model (LLM) based in-context learning (ICL) methods. Quantitative and qualitative experiments demonstrate that our proposed models achieve a higher macro F1 score compared to the baseline models on two scientific paper datasets, with improvements of 3.71% and 2.67%, respectively. The LLM based ICL methods are found to be not suitable for the task of problem and method extraction.

item.title: Detect, Unlearn, Restore: Defending Text Summarization Models Against Data Poisoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26036v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

在抽象式文本摘要任务中，大语言模型（LLM）常通过小规模任务特定数据集进行微调，但此类细粒度微调极易受训练期数据投毒攻击——攻击者仅需篡改少量样本，即可诱导模型持续生成偏见性、有害性或事实性错误的摘要，同时巧妙规避ROUGE等标准评估指标的异常检测。

方法创新

本文提出“检测—遗忘—恢复”（Detect, Unlearn, Restore）三位一体的后置式防御框架，覆盖机器学习供应链全流程：

  • 白盒检测：基于影响函数（influence function）量化样本训练影响力，结合语义一致性校验（如摘要与原文事实对齐度），识别异常高影响的投毒样本；
  • 黑盒审计：无需访问训练数据，通过向输入文档施加语义保持扰动（如同义替换、句式重组），观测模型输出敏感性——投毒模型敏感性达正常模型的2–3倍；
  • 梯度上升式遗忘：设计目标导向的梯度上升优化，精准削弱投毒样本对应的参数记忆，避免全局微调导致的性能塌陷。

关键发现与效果

在涵盖9种主流架构（如BART、T5、Pegasus）与6个基准数据集（CNN/DM、XSum等）的自适应攻击实验中：

  • 检测精度达85–92%，显著优于基线方法；
  • 遗忘后模型恢复96%原始行为，ROUGE-L仅下降<0.6%，实用性远超全量重训练；
  • 首次揭示投毒会引发持久性结构痕迹（如注意力头异常激活、嵌入空间偏移），为后门检测提供新理论依据。

本工作突破了传统投毒防御依赖训练数据或模型重训的局限，为部署级摘要系统提供了可落地、低开销的安全增强方案。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Detect, Unlearn, Restore—a unified post-hoc defense framework against fine-tuning–time data poisoning in abstractive text summarization. We expose persistent structural artifacts left by poisoning: poisoned samples exhibit abnormally high influence (white-box) and poisoned models show 2–3× higher sensitivity to semantics-preserving input perturbations (black-box). Our method achieves 85–92% detection precision across nine architectures and six benchmarks under adaptive attacks; gradient-ascent–based unlearning restores up to 96% of original behavior with <0.6% ROUGE degradation—eliminating the need for full retraining. The work establishes that fine-tuning poisoning is detectable and reversible without access to training data or model weights, enabling practical, deployment-ready security for summarization systems.

Abstract

Training-time data poisoning during fine-tuning poses a significant threat to large language models (LLMs) deployed for abstractive text summarization, where small task-specific datasets exert disproportionate influence on model behavior. In this setting, adversaries manipulate fine-tuning data to induce persistent summarization failures, such as biased or harmful summaries, while preserving standard evaluation metrics. We present a unified post-hoc defense framework for detecting and remediating fine-tuning-stage poisoning in summarization models across the machine learning supply chain. Our experiments show that in white-box settings, poisoned document-summary pairs exhibit abnormally high training influence, enabling detection via influence-function analysis with semantic consistency checks. In black-box settings, poisoned models display two to three times greater sensitivity to semantics-preserving perturbations, enabling behavioral auditing without training data access. Beyond existing poisoning formulations, we introduce novel attacks targeting factual distortion and representational bias, showing that poisoning alters summarization behavior without triggering conventional alarms. Across nine architectures and six benchmark datasets under adaptive attacks, our defenses achieve 85-92% detection precision, while gradient-ascent unlearning restores up to 96% of original behavior with minimal utility loss (less than 0.6% ROUGE degradation). These results indicate that fine-tuning-time poisoning leaves persistent structural artifacts, enabling practical detection and post-deployment recovery without full retraining.

item.title: Privacy Vulnerabilities of Attention Layers in Tabular Foundation Models and Protection of High-Risk Queries
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26021v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐私漏洞揭示与高风险查询防护：表格基础模型中注意力层的成员推断风险

传统观点认为，基于大规模合成数据预训练的表格基础模型（Tabular Foundation Models）隐私风险较低。然而，此类模型广泛依赖上下文内学习（in-context learning），即在推理阶段直接将真实敏感记录作为带标签的上下文示例输入——这构成了未被充分重视的隐私泄露通道。本文首次系统揭示：Transformer 的注意力机制本身即构成强隐私泄漏源。我们提出 AMIA（基于注意力的成员推断攻击）——一种无需影子模型、仅利用注意力权重分布集中性（attention concentration）的新型攻击方法。实验表明，注意力模式蕴含远超传统置信度分数的成员信号，在低误报率（FPR<0.1）区间，AMIA 相比最优置信度攻击平均提升7.7% 攻击准确率（AUC），证实其本质性威胁。

为防御该风险，我们设计了一种推理时轻量级保护机制，受 k-匿名性启发：通过语义感知的上下文键（context-key）表示压缩，降低其唯一性，不引入随机噪声、不修改模型参数、不需重训练。该机制仅对 AMIA 评分高的“高风险查询”触发，实现精准防护：对 AMIA 攻击的成员泄露抑制达50%，对置信度攻击亦达25%，同时仅造成3.9% 的预测性能下降（如 AUC）。进一步发现，微调（fine-tuning）会加剧隐私风险：当样本在微调后预测置信度显著上升时，其成员可推断性同步增强，表明微调可能放大模型对敏感训练样本的记忆，并通过置信度偏移暴露隐私信息。

AI Summary (English)

Tabular foundation models (TFMs), often pre-trained on synthetic data, are widely assumed to pose limited privacy risks. However, their reliance on in-context learning—where sensitive real records serve as labeled context examples at inference—creates a critical vulnerability. We demonstrate that attention patterns in transformer layers leak sufficient information for highly effective Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs). To exploit this, we propose AMIA, a shadow-model-free attack leveraging attention concentration, achieving +7.7% average AUC gain over state-of-the-art confidence-based MIAs, especially under low false-positive regimes. To mitigate this, we introduce an inference-time defense inspired by k-anonymity: it selectively compresses context-key representations of high-risk queries (identified by AMIA scores), reducing uniqueness without noise, retraining, or utility loss—yielding 50% reduction in AMIA leakage and 25% against confidence attacks, with only 3.9% predictive degradation. Crucially, we further show that fine-tuning amplifies privacy risk: samples exhibiting increased prediction confidence post-fine-tuning become significantly more vulnerable to MIAs, indicating fine-tuning strengthens memorization and leaks training data via confidence shifts.

Abstract

Tabular foundation models are commonly assumed to present limited privacy concerns as they are often pre-trained on large collections of synthetic data. However, these models leverage in-context learning, where sensitive records may be provided directly at inference time as labelled context examples. In this paper, we demonstrate that predictions generated via the attention mechanism leak sufficient information to enable effective Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs). To highlight this vulnerability, we propose AMIA (Attention-based Membership Inference Attack), a shadow-model-free attack that exploits the concentration of transformer attention patterns. Our results show that attention mechanisms reveal strong membership signals, which exceed classical confidence-based attacks, achieving an average gain of 7.7\%, specially in low false-positive regimes. To mitigate this risk, we introduce an inference-time defence inspired by $k$-anonymity principles. This approach reduces the uniqueness of context-key representations without introducing random noise or retraining the model. By targeting only high-risk queries identified through AMIA scores, the defence substantially reduces membership leakage of this attack by an average of 50\% and 25\% against confidence-based attacks, while preserving predictive utility with only 3.9\% performance degradation. Beyond showing that context examples are vulnerable, we further demonstrate that fine-tuning introduces an additional source of privacy risk. In particular, samples whose prediction confidence increases after fine-tuning become more susceptible to MIAs, indicating that fine-tuning can amplify memorisation and expose sensitive training information through confidence shifts.

item.title: The Web4 Agent Economy: A Large-Scale Empirical Study of the Landscape, Challenges, and Opportunities
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25876v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

互联网正从Web3迈向Web4阶段，其核心特征是自主智能体（Autonomous Agents）作为独立经济主体参与链上与链下协作。这些代理可持有加密钱包、执行链上交易、调用外部API并完成跨环境支付，催生了对新型基础设施的迫切需求——包括代理-工具交互、代理间支付、可验证代理身份等关键能力。Model Context Protocol（MCP）、x402、EIP-8004等新兴协议正试图构建这一基础层，但工业界热度与实证研究严重失衡：真实世界中Web4代理生态的规模、实践模式与瓶颈尚属空白。

方法与数据

本研究首次开展大规模实证分析，覆盖99,448个多链身份注册记录、3.17亿条链上交易日志、341个MCP开源项目源码及349个经筛选的GitHub问题报告，系统回答三大问题：（1）Web4代理在生产环境中的部署架构与使用模式；（2）开发者构建代理时面临的核心工程挑战；（3）社区对挑战的响应机制与有效性。

主要发现

  • 已形成高活跃度机器对机器（M2M）支付经济：日均处理数百万笔代理间交易，但底层基础设施严重不成熟：身份/授权实践碎片化、跨环境（链上/云/API）操作缺乏统一范式、支付协议互操作性缺失。
  • 社区响应可见但高度不均衡：仅17%的仓库具备系统性错误处理文档，支付互操作性问题在GitHub Issue中占比达41%，且平均修复周期超112天，成为最顽固瓶颈。
  • 本研究揭示了Web4代理经济“繁荣表象”与“脆弱基座”的根本张力，为构建安全、可扩展、互操作的Web4代理基础设施提供了首个实证基准与优先级路线图。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale empirical study of the emerging Web4 agent economy—the ecosystem where autonomous agents operate as independent economic actors on and off-chain. We analyze 99,448 multi-chain identity registrations, 317.6M transaction logs, source code from 341 MCP projects, and 349 curated GitHub issues to characterize real-world deployment patterns, engineering challenges, and community responses. Our findings reveal a highly active machine-to-machine payment economy processing millions of daily transactions—yet built atop immature infrastructure: fragmented identity/authorization practices, ad-hoc cross-environment operation, and critically, poor payment protocol interoperability. Community engagement is observable but uneven; payment interoperability remains the most persistent bottleneck, accounting for 41% of high-severity issues with median resolution time exceeding 112 days. This work exposes a critical gap between rapid economic growth and fragile technical foundations, providing actionable insights for building a secure, scalable, and interoperable Web4 agent infrastructure.

Abstract

The Internet is transitioning from Web3 toward Web4, where autonomous agents serve as independent economic actors. These agents can now hold crypto wallets, execute on-chain trades, and pay for external API calls. This transition calls for a new infrastructure stack capable of supporting key agent operations, including agent-to-tool interaction, agent-to-agent payments, and verifiable agent identity, represented by emerging protocols such as the Model Context Protocol, x402, and EIP-8004. Despite growing industrial interest in these protocols, the real-world Web4 agent ecosystem remains largely underexplored. To bridge this gap, we conduct the first large-scale empirical study of the Web4 ecosystem. Specifically, our study targets three interconnected questions: how Web4 agents are deployed and used in practice; what engineering challenges developers face when building Web4 agents; how current project communities respond to these challenges. To answer these questions, we analyze 99,448 multi-chain identity registrations, 317,596,323 transaction logs, the source code of 341 MCP projects, and 349 filtered GitHub issues. Our findings reveal that autonomous agents have established a highly active machine-to-machine payment economy, processing millions of daily transactions. However, this growth is built on immature infrastructure, including identity/authorization practice, cross-environment operation, and payment interoperability. Our follow-up analysis shows that community responses are visible but unevenly distributed across repositories, and payment interoperability remains the most persistent unresolved bottleneck. Overall, this study reveals a critical gap between the rapid growth of the Web4 agent economy and its fragile underlying infrastructure, highlighting future directions for building a more secure Web4 agent ecosystem.

item.title: Color Matters: Trigger Color Affects Success in Federated Backdoor Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25858v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）因数据隐私保护优势被广泛应用，但其分布式架构易受后门攻击威胁。现有研究多关注触发器形状、位置或注入方式，却忽视了触发器颜色这一基础视觉属性对攻击效能的潜在影响。本文首次系统探究“语义驱动型”后门攻击中触发色（trigger color） 的关键作用——在保持触发语义（如口罩、墨镜）、空间位置及投毒预算不变的前提下，仅改变触发器的色彩（黑白二值化），评估其对攻击成功率的调控效应。

方法设计

我们构建了一个可控FL实验框架：以CelebA数据集上的四类发色分类任务为基准，恶意客户端将源类图像叠加语义触发物（如白色/黑色口罩），并重标为攻击者指定的目标类；良性客户端仅使用干净数据训练。攻击优化目标采用双层级设计：（1）标准投毒目标（最小化触发样本的误分类损失）；（2）增强型SABLE目标——联合优化：①干净样本分类损失、②触发样本目标类损失、③倒数第二层特征空间中目标类与源类的特征分离损失、④恶意更新与全局模型的L2正则化项，以抑制更新漂移、提升隐蔽性。

主要发现与创新点

实验表明：触发色显著影响攻击成功率——白触发器对“金发”目标类攻击成功率提升达23.7%，黑触发器对“黑发”目标类提升19.5%；该趋势在鲁棒聚合机制（如Krum、Median）下依然稳健。这揭示：颜色不仅是表观属性，更是触发器与目标语义间感知一致性的关键媒介，在FL后门机制的设计、持久性分析与评估中具有不可替代性。本研究为语义后门的精细化建模提供了新维度，也为防御策略（如基于颜色敏感性的检测）指明方向。

AI Summary (English)

This paper reveals that trigger color—a previously overlooked visual attribute—is a decisive factor in semantic backdoor attacks against federated learning (FL).
